name: Beef with Broccoli
recipeInstructions:
1. Carefully slice beef into thin bite-size pieces across the grain.
Marinate the beef in soya sauce, wine, and salt.
2. Shred the broccoli into 3-inch lengths.
3. Place oil in a wok and heat to smoking point. Quickly stir-fry the beef for no more than 3 minutes, then remove it to a platter and reserve.
4. Add the shredded broccoli, 1/2 cup water, and sugar to the wok. Turn the burner to medium-high heat, cover the broccoli, and cook for 6 minutes.
Stir the broccoli occasionally by lifting the cover of the wok to ensure even cooking on both sides.
5. At the end of 6 minutes, the broccoli should be just cooked through. If not, continue cooking for an additional minute or two.
6. Add the reserved beef back to the broccoli and stir everything together. Place the mixture in a platter.
NOTE: Pork or chicken may be substituted by following the same steps but do not remove pork or chicken from the wok after initial stir-frying; instead, continue to cook it with the broccoli. Beef is removed for reserve because it toughens with prolonged cooking.
